flashcards.front: What is the String Abstract Data Type (ADT)?
flashcards.back: The String ADT is a formal definition of a string as a finite sequence of characters, along with operations such as StrLen, StrConcat, SubStr, StrCompare, StrInsert, StrDelete, and StrIndex.

flashcards.front: What are the three major string representations?
flashcards.back:
1. Fixed-Length Array (C-style Null-Terminated)
2. Variable-Length with Dynamic Allocation
3. Linked List Representation

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of naive pattern matching?
flashcards.back: The time complexity of naive pattern matching is O(n * m) in the worst case, where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern.

flashcards.front: How does the KMP algorithm improve upon naive pattern matching?
flashcards.back: The KMP algorithm uses a failure function to avoid redundant comparisons, resulting in a time complexity of O(n + m).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the failure function in the KMP algorithm?
flashcards.back: The failure function determines the next position to compare in the text, based on the longest proper prefix of the pattern that is also a suffix.

flashcards.front: What are the trade-offs between the three string representations?
flashcards.back:
Fixed-Length Array: O(1) access, O(n) concatenation, may waste space
Variable-Length Array: O(1) access, O(n) concatenation, may require reallocation
Linked List: O(n) access, O(1) concatenation, high overhead

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a null-terminated string and a dynamically allocated string?
flashcards.back: A null-terminated string is an array of characters with a null character at the end, while a dynamically allocated string is a block of memory allocated at runtime to store a string.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the StrLen operation in the String ADT?
flashcards.back: The StrLen operation returns the length of a string, excluding the null terminator.
